icebreakers.name: Two Truths and a Lie
icebreakers.description: Each person shares two true facts and one lie about themselves. Others guess which is the lie.
icebreakers.instructions: Have each person take turns sharing two true facts and one lie about themselves. The rest of the group tries to guess which statement is the lie. After everyone guesses, reveal the truth!

icebreakers.name: Personal Coat of Arms
icebreakers.description: Create a personal coat of arms representing your values, interests, and personality.
icebreakers.instructions: Give everyone paper and markers. Ask them to create a personal coat of arms with 4 sections: something they're proud of, a hobby they love, a goal they have, and something that makes them unique. Share and explain your designs.

icebreakers.name: Speed Networking
icebreakers.description: Quick one-on-one conversations to get to know each other better.
icebreakers.instructions: Pair people up for 2-minute conversations. After each round, one person moves to the next partner. Use prompts like 'What's something you're excited about this week?' or 'What's a skill you'd love to learn?'

icebreakers.name: Childhood Memory Share
icebreakers.description: Share a favorite childhood memory and what it taught you.
icebreakers.instructions: Go around the group and have each person share a favorite childhood memory. Ask them to reflect on what that memory taught them or why it's special to them.

icebreakers.name: Energy Ball Toss
icebreakers.description: Pass an imaginary energy ball around the circle with different energy levels.
icebreakers.instructions: Stand in a circle. Pass an imaginary 'energy ball' around with different energy levels - slow and heavy, fast and light, bouncy, etc. Each person adds their own energy style before passing it on.

icebreakers.name: Name and Gesture
icebreakers.description: Introduce yourself with your name and a unique gesture that everyone repeats.
icebreakers.instructions: Each person says their name while doing a unique gesture. Everyone else repeats both the name and gesture. Go around the circle until everyone has introduced themselves.

icebreakers.name: Quick Draw Challenge
icebreakers.description: Draw something in 30 seconds while others guess what it is.
icebreakers.instructions: Give everyone paper and a pen. Set a timer for 30 seconds. One person draws something (keep it secret) while others try to guess. Rotate who draws each round.

icebreakers.name: Would You Rather
icebreakers.description: Quick fun questions to get people talking and laughing.
icebreakers.instructions: Ask rapid-fire 'Would you rather' questions like 'Would you rather have the ability to fly or be invisible?' or 'Would you rather always be 10 minutes late or 20 minutes early?'

icebreakers.name: Team Values Discussion
icebreakers.description: Discuss and align on team values and working styles.
icebreakers.instructions: Have each person share what they value most in teamwork (communication, creativity, structure, etc.). Discuss how these values can guide your team's work together. Create a shared list of team values.

icebreakers.name: Future Visioning
icebreakers.description: Collaboratively envision and align on team goals and direction.
icebreakers.instructions: Give everyone sticky notes. Ask them to write down their vision for the team in 6 months. Share and group similar ideas. Discuss how to work toward these shared goals together.
